channel.title: Silicon Labs — recent CVEs
channel.description: Most recent CVEs assigned by Silicon Labs (silabs).

item.title: CVE-2026-4930 · 7.1 HIGH — DPA Countermeasures weakening on Series 3 devices
item.description: SYMCRYPTO is the SiXG301's host side hardware engine accessed by PSA crypto library that accelerates symmetric cryptographic operations (AES encryption/decryption and hashing). DPA Countermeasures on SYMCRYPTO can be weakened (reduced entropy) by for…

item.title: CVE-2026-6432 · 5.3 MEDIUM — Improper bounds validation in EmberZNet SDK
item.description: Improper bounds validation in EmberZNet SDK versions 9.0.2 and earlier may result in crashes or dynamic memory leakage.

item.title: CVE-2026-47154 · 7.1 HIGH — Simple Metering GetProfileResponse interval-bounds bug in EmberZNet v9.0.2
item.description: In EmberZNet v9.0.2 and earlier, a malformed GetProfileResponse message can trigger out-of-bounds reads while iterating interval entries and terminate the process. These messages must come from a device that has already joined the network, and no inf…

item.title: CVE-2026-47153 · 7.1 HIGH — Level Control Step With On/Off divide-by-zero in EmberZNet v9.0.2
item.description: In EmberZNet v9.0.2 and earlier, a malformed Level Control Step command can terminate the process through a divide-by-zero fault. This command must come from a device that has already joined the network. Only devices supporting the Level Control clus…

item.title: CVE-2026-47152 · 7.1 HIGH — Level Control Move divide-by-zero in EmberZNet v9.0.2
item.description: In EmberZNet v9.0.2 and earlier, a malformed Level Control Move command can terminate the process through a divide-by-zero fault. This command must come from a device that has already joined the network. Only devices supporting the Level Control clus…

item.title: CVE-2026-47151 · 7.1 HIGH — Door Lock ClearWeekdaySchedule invalid table index and write in EmberZNet v9.0.2
item.description: In EmberZNet v9.0.2 and earlier, malformed ClearWeekdaySchedule messages can trigger out-of-bounds writes into Door Lock schedule state. The size and location of this data is limited. These messages must come from a device that has already joined the…

item.title: CVE-2026-47150 · 7.1 HIGH — IAS Zone enroll invalid table index and write in EmberZNet 9.0.2
item.description: In EmberZNet v9.0.2 and earlier, malformed IAS Zone enrollment messages can trigger an out-of-bounds state-table write and terminate the process. The size and location of this write is limited. These messages must come from a device that has already…

item.title: CVE-2026-47149 · 7.1 HIGH — Door Lock GetUserType invalid table index in EmberZNet v9.0.2
item.description: In EmberZNet v9.0.2 and earlier, malformed or out-of-range Door Lock user identifiers can trigger out-of-bounds table reads and terminate the process. These messages must come from a device that has already joined the network, and no information leak…

item.title: CVE-2026-47148 · 7.1 HIGH — Groups GetGroupMembership count/list-length mismatch in EmberZNet v9.0.2
item.description: In EmberZNet v9.0.2 and earlier, malformed GetGroupMembership commands can trigger repeated reads past the end of the message payload and terminate the process. These messages must come from a device that has already joined the network, and no inform…

item.title: CVE-2026-47147 · 7.1 HIGH — OTA server raw parser missing per-field bounds validation in EmberZNet v9.0.2
item.description: In EmberZNet v9.0.2 and earlier, malformed OTA requests can drive the OTA server parser into out-of-bounds reads. A limited amount of data from RAM is read back to the requester. The size and location of this data is limited. These requests must come…

item.title: CVE-2026-47146 · 7.1 HIGH — Color Control color-temperature assertion abort in EmberZNet v9.0.2
item.description: In EmberZNet v9.0.2 and earlier, malformed Color Control messages can lead to asserts that terminate the process. These messages must come from a device that has already joined the network. Only devices supporting the Color Control cluster may be imp…

item.title: CVE-2026-47145 · 7.1 HIGH — Color Control hue/saturation assertion abort in EmberZNet v9.0.2
item.description: In EmberZNet v9.0.2 and earlier, malformed Color Control messages can lead to asserts that terminate the process. These messages must come from a device that has already joined the network. Only devices supporting the Color Control cluster may be imp…

item.title: CVE-2026-4526 · 7.1 HIGH — Global ZCL command parser missing minimum-length validation in EmberZNet v9.0.2
item.description: In EmberZNet v9.0.2 and earlier, malformed global ZCL messages can trigger out-of-bounds reads in framework parsing logic and terminate the process. These messages must come from a device that has already joined the network, and no information leakag…

item.title: CVE-2026-2815 · 8.4 HIGH — Incorrect use of the PUF key for user key generation in EFR32xG27 results in predictable keys
item.description: Incorrect use of the PUF key for user key generation in EFR32xG27 results in predictable keys

item.title: CVE-2026-8676 · 8.8 HIGH
item.description: An attacker is able to downgrade the security of a Bluetooth LE connection by deleting an existing bond, spoofing the bonded device and creating a new bond.

item.title: CVE-2025-14972 · 4.1 MEDIUM — Insufficient DPA countermeasure reseeding
item.description: * Countermeasures for DPA within SYMCRYPTO engine on SixG301xxx devices are not sufficiently random and will eventually repeat. * KSU keys using SYMCRYPTO will be impacted by this vulnerability.

item.title: CVE-2026-3290 · 7.4 HIGH — Timing limitations of the HRNG in RS9116 when power save mode is enabled results in predictable values
item.description: Timing limitations of the HRNG in RS9116 when power save mode is enabled results in predictable values

item.title: CVE-2025-11571 · 2.1 LOW 🌩️ — Command Execution vulnerability in Simplicity Installer
item.description: Vulnerable endpoints accept user-controlled input through a URL in JSON format which enables command execution. The commands allowed to execute can open executables. However, the commands cannot pass parameters or arguments. To successfully execute t…

item.title: CVE-2025-14055 · 2.4 LOW — Integer underflow in Secure NCP host
item.description: An integer underflow vulnerability in Silicon Labs Secure NCP host implementation allows a buffer overread via a specially crafted packet.

item.title: CVE-2025-14547 · 2.3 LOW — ECJ-PAKE Integer Underflow Vulnerability in Silicon Labs PSA Crypto and SE Manager APIs
item.description: An integer underflow vulnerability is present in Silicon Lab’s implementation of PSA Crypto and SE Manager EC-JPAKE APIs during ZKP parsing. Triggering the underflow can lead to a hard fault, causing a temporary denial of service.

item.title: CVE-2026-0619 · 6 MEDIUM — Integer Wraparound DoS in Silicon Labs Matter Implementation
item.description: A reachable infinite loop via an integer wraparound is present in Silicon Labs' Matter SDK which allows an attacker to trigger a denial of service. A hard reset is required to recover the device.

item.title: CVE-2025-11004 · 7.5 HIGH 🌩️ — Reflected XSS vulnerability in Simplicity Device Manager tool
item.description: The Simplicity Device Manager Tool has a Reflected XSS (Cross-site-scripting) vulnerability in several API endpoints. The attacker needs to be on the same network to execute this attack. These APIs can affect confidentiality, integrity, and availabil…

item.title: CVE-2025-7432 · 1 LOW — DPA countermeasures not reseeded under certain conditions
item.description: DPA countermeasures in Silicon Labs' Series 2 devices are not reseeded under certain conditions. This may allow an attacker to eventually extract secret keys through a DPA attack.

item.title: CVE-2025-12131 · 5.3 MEDIUM — Truncated 802.15.4 packet leads to denial of service
item.description: A truncated 802.15.4 packet can lead to an assert, resulting in a denial of service.

item.title: CVE-2025-7964 · 9.2 CRITICAL 🌩️ — Zigbee Router Denial of Service
item.description: After receiving a malformed 802.15.4 MAC Data Request the Zigbee Coordinator sends a ‘network leave’ request to Zigbee router resulting in the Zigbee Router getting stuck in a non-rejoinable state. If a suitable parent is not available, the end devic…

item.title: CVE-2025-10933 · 5.3 MEDIUM — Silicon Labs Z-Wave Protocol Controller Integer underflow vulnerability leads to out of bounds read
item.description: An integer underflow vulnerability in the Silicon Labs Z-Wave Protocol Controller can lead to out of bounds memory reads.

item.title: CVE-2025-12986 · 6 MEDIUM — Denial of Service Vulnerability in Silicon Labs WF200 and WGM160P Devices
item.description: When a WF200/WGM160P device is configured to operate as an Access Point, it may be vulnerable to a denial of service triggered by a malformed packet. The device may recover automatically or require a hard reset.

item.title: CVE-2025-10285 · 7.4 HIGH — Simplcity Device Manager exposes NTLMv2 hash
item.description: The web interface of the Silicon Labs Simplicity Device Manager is exposed publicly and can be used to extract the NTLMv2 hash which an attacker could use to crack the user's domain password.

item.title: CVE-2025-4321 · 7.1 HIGH — DoS in RS9116W-WiSeConnect L2CAP protocol due to reception of malformed packets
item.description: In a Bluetooth device, using RS9116-WiseConnect SDK experiences a Denial of Service, if it receives malformed L2CAP packets, only hard reset will bring the device to normal operation

item.title: CVE-2025-10693 · 7.6 HIGH 🌩️ — Silicon Labs Z-Wave PIR Sensor Joins Network as Non-Secure
item.description: When SmartStart Inclusion fails during the onboarding of a Z-Wave PIR sensor, the sensor will join the network as a non-secure device. This vulnerability exists in Silicon Labs' Z-Wave PIR Sensor Reference design delivered as part of SiSDK v2025.6.0…

item.title: CVE-2025-8414 · 9.4 CRITICAL — Zigbee Green Power Host Buffer Overflow Vulnerability
item.description: Due to improper input validation, a buffer overflow vulnerability is present in Zigbee EZSP Host Applications. If the buffer overflows, stack corruption is possible. In certain conditions, this could lead to arbitrary code execution. Access to a netw…

item.title: CVE-2025-7448 · 8.6 HIGH — Man in the middle (MitM) attack vulnerability in Wi-SUN library
item.description: Wi-SUN unexpected 4- Way Handshake packet receptions may lead to predictable keys and potentially leading to Man in the middle (MitM) attack

item.title: CVE-2025-1394 · 5.9 MEDIUM — Denial of Service (DoS) vulnerabilitiey in Zigbee library
item.description: The Ember ZNet stack’s packet buffer manager may read out of bound memory leading to an assert, causing a Denial of Service (DoS).

item.title: CVE-2025-1221 · 5.9 MEDIUM — DoS in Zigbee device due to heavy traffic
item.description: A Zigbee Radio Co-Processor (RCP), which is using SiLabs EmberZNet Zigbee stack, was unable to send messages to the host system (CPCd) due to heavy Zigbee traffic, resulting in a Denial of Service (DoS) attack, Only hard reset will bring the device t…

item.title: CVE-2025-2329 · 5.3 MEDIUM — High traffic causes corrupt SPI packets in OpenThread leading to denial of service
item.description: In high traffic environments, a Silicon Labs OpenThread RCP (see impacted versions) fails to clear the SPI transmit buffer and may send a corrupt packet over SPI to its host, causing the host to reset the RCP which results in a denial of service.

item.title: CVE-2025-3873 · 6 MEDIUM — Buffer overflow in Si91x crypto APIs
item.description: The following APIs for the Silcon Labs SiWx91x prior to vesion 3.4.0 failed to check the size of the output buffer of the caller which could lead to data corruption on the host (Cortex-M4) application. sl_si91x_aes sl_si91x_gcm sl_si91x_ccm sl_si91x_…

item.title: CVE-2025-3301 · 1 LOW — DPA Countermeasures Unavailable for Certain Cryptographic Operations on Series 2 Devices
item.description: DPA countermeasures are unavailable for ECDH key agreement and EdDSA signing operations on Curve25519 and Curve448 on all Series 2 modules and SoCs due to a lack of hardware and software support. A successful DPA attack may result in exposure of conf…

item.title: CVE-2024-9055 · 4.2 MEDIUM — DPA Countermeasures need reseeding
item.description: The DPA countermeasures on Silicon Labs' Series 2 devices are not reseeded periodically as they should be. This may allow an attacker to eventually extract secret keys through a DPA attack.

item.title: CVE-2024-12975 · 1 LOW — Silicon Labs CPC can leak information in full duplex SPI
item.description: A buffer overread can occur in the CPC application when operating in full duplex SPI upon receiving an invalid packet over the SPI interface.

item.title: CVE-2024-6351 · 4.3 MEDIUM — Malformed packet leads to denial of service in NWK/APS layer
item.description: A malformed packet can cause a buffer overflow in the NWK/APS layer of the Ember ZNet stack and lead to an assert

item.title: CVE-2024-9499 · 8.6 HIGH — Uncontrolled search path can lead to DLL hijacking in USBXpress Win 98SE Dev Kit installer
item.description: DLL hijacking vulnerabilities, caused by an uncontrolled search path in the USBXpress Win 98SE Dev Kit installer can lead to privilege escalation and arbitrary code execution when running the impacted installer.

item.title: CVE-2024-9498 · 8.6 HIGH — Uncontrolled search path can lead to DLL hijacking in USBXpress SDK installer
item.description: DLL hijacking vulnerabilities, caused by an uncontrolled search path in the USBXpress SDK installer can lead to privilege escalation and arbitrary code execution when running the impacted installer.

item.title: CVE-2024-9497 · 8.6 HIGH — Uncontrolled search path can lead to DLL hijacking in USBXpress 4 SDK installer
item.description: DLL hijacking vulnerabilities, caused by an uncontrolled search path in the USBXpress 4 SDK installer can lead to privilege escalation and arbitrary code execution when running the impacted installer.

item.title: CVE-2024-9496 · 8.6 HIGH — Uncontrolled search path can lead to DLL hijacking in USBXpress Dev Kit installer
item.description: DLL hijacking vulnerabilities, caused by an uncontrolled search path in the USBXpress Dev Kit installer can lead to privilege escalation and arbitrary code execution when running the impacted installer.

item.title: CVE-2024-9495 · 8.6 HIGH — Uncontrolled search path can lead to DLL hijacking in CP210x VCP Windows installer
item.description: DLL hijacking vulnerabilities, caused by an uncontrolled search path in the CP210x VCP Windows installer can lead to privilege escalation and arbitrary code execution when running the impacted installer.

item.title: CVE-2024-9494 · 8.6 HIGH — Uncontrolled search path can lead to DLL hijacking in CP210 VCP Win 2k installer
item.description: DLL hijacking vulnerabilities, caused by an uncontrolled search path in the CP210 VCP Win 2k installer can lead to privilege escalation and arbitrary code execution when running the impacted installer.

item.title: CVE-2024-9493 · 8.6 HIGH — Uncontrolled search path can lead to DLL hijacking in ToolStick installer
item.description: DLL hijacking vulnerabilities, caused by an uncontrolled search path in the ToolStick installer can lead to privilege escalation and arbitrary code execution when running the impacted installer.

item.title: CVE-2024-9492 · 8.6 HIGH — Uncontrolled search path can lead to DLL hijacking in Flash Programming Utility installer
item.description: DLL hijacking vulnerabilities, caused by an uncontrolled search path in Flash Programming Utility installer can lead to privilege escalation and arbitrary code execution when running the impacted installer.

item.title: CVE-2024-9491 · 8.6 HIGH — Uncontrolled search path can lead to DLL hijacking in Configuration Wizard 2 installer
item.description: DLL hijacking vulnerabilities, caused by an uncontrolled search path in Configuration Wizard 2 installer can lead to privilege escalation and arbitrary code execution when running the impacted installer.

item.title: CVE-2024-9490 · 8.6 HIGH — Uncontrolled search path can lead to DLL hijacking in Silicon Labs IDE installer
item.description: DLL hijacking vulnerabilities, caused by an uncontrolled search path in Silicon Labs (8-bit) IDE installer can lead to privilege escalation and arbitrary code execution when running the impacted installer.
